canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, WMT19 is consistently described as a benchmark or dataset from the Workshop on Machine Translation 2019. Its prevailing use is to evaluate the performance of machine translation systems, as it is explicitly positioned as a measure for the behavior of "Machine translation". The benchmark is operationalized in a few different ways across the surveyed papers. Some studies utilize a range of its language pairs, such as "De–En, Fi–En, Lt–En, and Ru–En", for general translation evaluation. A more specific application is also documented, where one paper uses "the ‘Kazakh-English’ subset" to assess the "translation of minority languages" in the context of fine-tuning. The definitions are largely in agreement, framing it as a tool for evaluating machine translation, with variations in its application from broad performance testing to more targeted analyses on specific data subsets.
papers: Seeing Through Words, Speaking Through Pixels: Deep Representational Alignment Between Vision and Language Models
XAutoLM: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Language Models via Meta-Learning andAutoML
STAFF: Speculative Coreset Selection for Task-Specific Fine-tuning
Uncertainty-Aware Decoding with Minimum Bayes Risk

definitions.text: The Workshop on Machine Translation 2019 benchmark, used to evaluate machine translation systems.
definitions.paper: Uncertainty-Aware Decoding with Minimum Bayes Risk

definitions.text: A machine translation dataset, here using the Kazakh-English subset, used to evaluate fine-tuning performance after coreset selection.
definitions.paper: STAFF: Speculative Coreset Selection for Task-Specific Fine-tuning

relationships.paper: Uncertainty-Aware Decoding with Minimum Bayes Risk
relationships.evidence: We use WMT14 (Bojar et al., 2014), IWSLT14 (Cettolo et al., 2014), afroMT (Reid et al., 2021), IWSLT17 (Cettolo et al., 2017), WMT18 (Bojar et al., 2018), and WMT19 (Barrault et al., 2019) for machine translation (Section 4.1)

iclr25: Uncertainty-Aware Decoding with Minimum Bayes Risk
emnlp25: XAutoLM: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Language Models via Meta-Learning andAutoML
